description: In September 2008, the U.S. House of Representatives initially rejected the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP), a $700 billion bank bailout proposed by the Bush administration. Public outrage was immediate and vocal, forcing Congress to reconsider and pass a revised version just days later.

key_events.description: House of Representatives defeats initial TARP bill, causing stock market crash.
Intense lobbying from Wall Street and White House shifts political discourse.
Revised TARP bill passes both chambers of Congress.

calibration_notes: Provides a case study on rapid political pivot in response to systemic market collapse and public pressure.
